label: Battery Controller v2
info:
Cost-optimized battery charge/discharge controller.
Queries InfluxDB for prices, solar forecast, SOC, and load.
Outputs optimal state for each 15-min price interval.

name: AC Load
query: SELECT LAST(acload) AS acload FROM ess WHERE time > now() - 30m

name: Power
query: SELECT LAST(Power) AS power FROM ess WHERE time > now() - 30m

name: Cache & topic: prices
Solar
query: SELECT sunshineDurationInMinutes FROM forecastnew WHERE time >= now() - 1h ORDER BY time ASC

name: Load History
query: SELECT mean(acload) AS avg_load FROM ess WHERE time > now() - 7d GROUP BY time(1h) ORDER BY time ASC

name: Add Weather Context
Battery Optimizer
=== Battery Controller v2 - Main Flow ===
info:
Runs every 15 minutes.
1. Queries InfluxDB for SOC, prices, solar forecast, load
2. Optimizes battery state for each price interval
3. Writes plan to batterycontrol.plan
4. Outputs current setpoint

name: === Test Cases ===
info: Three test scenarios using historic/simulated data

name: Test 1: Sunny Apr 7 neg prices
Need fresh prices?
Retry if no tomorrow
Read cached prices
PV Now
query: SELECT LAST(PAC) AS pv_now FROM pv WHERE time > now() - 30m

name: PV Hist recent
query: SELECT mean(mean_PAC) AS avg_pv, max(mean_PAC) AS max_pv FROM pv WHERE time > now() - 15d GROUP BY time(1h) ORDER BY time ASC

name: PV Hist 1y ago
query: SELECT mean(mean_PAC) AS avg_pv, max(mean_PAC) AS max_pv FROM pv WHERE time > now() - 380d AND time < now() - 350d GROUP BY time(1h) ORDER BY time ASC

name: PV Hist 2y ago
query: SELECT mean(mean_PAC) AS avg_pv, max(mean_PAC) AS max_pv FROM pv WHERE time > now() - 745d AND time < now() - 715d GROUP BY time(1h) ORDER BY time ASC

name: Merge & topic: pv_history
=== Changelog ===
info:
**2026-06-28** - BMS-aware SOC guard. The optimizer now trusts the capacity-weighted BMS SOC (`global.bms`, published by the checkbattery `capture` node and surfaced via the "SOC from global" node) instead of the Victron aggregate (`global.ess.Soc`), which read 71.5% while the packs were really ~84% and grid-charged an almost-full battery after 16:00 at the full 13ct fee. Two safety nets in the optimizer: (1) range + physically-impossible-jump rejection (Δ beyond ~15%/h ⇒ socTrust=false); (2) per-pack full-stop — never grid-charge the LIVE slot if ANY single pack ≥ PACK_FULL_PCT (97%), since unequal parallel packs (bat_ost/bat_west 135Ah, bat_big 310Ah, differing SOH) fill at different rates and the small pack tops out first. Falls back to `global.ess.Soc` when BMS data is stale/absent. Both guards gate state=1 at i===0 only; future slots re-evaluate next tick.

**2026-06-25** - Phase 3b-arb (arbitrage grid-charge). On exceptional-delta days, grid-charge cheap slots SPECIFICALLY to resell at a high feed-in peak that lies before the next free PV refill — deliberately opening the grid→feed-in path the other phases block. Gated by a NET round-trip hurdle so it stays dormant on normal days: peakMp*ARB_RT_EFF − chargeEff − ARB_CYCLE_WEAR_CT ≥ ARB_MIN_NET_CT. Constants: ARB_MIN_NET_CT=16 (ct net/kWh), ARB_RT_EFF=0.9 (~10% round-trip loss), ARB_CYCLE_WEAR_CT=1.5, ARB_CHARGE_SOC_PCT=100 (arbitrage may fill to 100%, vs the normal 90% Phase 3c cap). Runs after preemptive, before Phase 3c; picks tagged _plan='charge' so existing SOC sims / state=1 emit / Phase 3d feed-in budget + round-trip checks resell the surplus at the peak automatically. Horizon-bounded to the next free PV refill (intraday round-trip only; cross-day bets left to the next cycle). User: charge from grid to feed in more energy and make profit when the intraday delta is exceptional.

**2026-06-17** - Cross-day feed-in hold (opportunity-cost floor). User flagged heavy feed-in today at ≤17ct while tomorrow's 46ct peak was skipped. Cause: Phase 3d feed-in only sees slots within the horizon and the round-trip check only guards grid REBUY cost; a single cheap PV slot tomorrow flips freeRefillAhead, draining the battery (86%→27%) on the false assumption PV refills it — but a cloudy tomorrow only reached ~45%, missing the peak. Fix: when in-schedule PV won't curtail (pvWillCurtail=false) and a materially higher stored-energy feed-in peak lies beyond the horizon, block tonight feed-in below (futurePeak − CROSSDAY_HOLD_SLACK_CT=3ct) so SOC is held for the better peak (captured next cycle as the horizon advances). Floor is not overflow-exempt here (any 'overflow' budget is post-schedule speculation). No-op when a real solar glut curtails before the peak. New regression Scenario 10.

**2026-06-11** - No grid-charge when battery already near-full. User flagged grid charges at ~6ct firing at 93-95% SOC on a sunny day (pre-loading a forecast-dark tomorrow). Cause: Phase 3c marks cheapest slots _plan='charge' using a SOC sim clamped at MAX_GRID_CHARGE_SOC_PCT (90%), but Phase 4 fired state=1 there unconditionally even when real PV had overshot the cap. Fix: runtime now skips a planned charge when soc >= 90% (compensate instead); profit charges (ep<0, paid to import) still fill.

**2026-06-08** - No discharge-today / charge-tomorrow round-trip. User flagged: on a sunny-today / no-sun-tomorrow boundary the plan fed stored solar to grid at tonight's evening peak (17:30-18:30, PV=0, mp ~18-19ct) draining ~14% SOC, then grid-charged tomorrow midday (mp ~2-7ct -> ~15-20ct effective with the 13ct fee) on the dark day - selling stored energy and rebuying it at retail (net loss after ~10% inverter round-trip + cycle wear). Cause: Phase 3d's `isOverflowOnly` curtailment exemption sized the free-energy budget from today's midday PV clipping but allocated it market-price-DESC, spending it on the evening peak instead of the midday slots where clipping physically occurs; the exemption then bypassed the round-trip check (mp 19.4 < replacement 16.3 + 5ct margin). Two compounding holes: (a) the curtailment ('overflow') exemption was sized from today's midday PV clipping but allocated market-price-DESC, so it exempted the evening peak (PV=0); (b) the round-trip check's replacement price used minReplacementEffPrice = the single globally-cheapest refill slot (tomorrow's 10:30 midday slot at mp 2.34 + summer-discounted 10.4ct fee = 12.74ct effective), letting tonight's 18ct feed-in pass although the real rebuy cost was the MARGINAL grid-charge slot (~22ct). Fix (a): an early (pre-grid-charge) slot is overflow-exempt only when it is itself a real PV-surplus slot (`s.pvPower > s.loadEst`). Fix (b): early-slot replacement price = max(minReplacementEffPrice, max effective among planned 'charge' slots) — the marginal refill cost. Both needed: (a) drops the evening slots to the round-trip check, (b) makes that check reject them (mp 18-19 < 22 + 5 margin). Late slots keep the next-day-PV-refill exemption (isLate); days with no planned grid-charge are unchanged (_marginalChargeEff = -Inf). Midday clipping still feeds in (free energy); evening stored-energy drains on dark-tomorrow days are blocked, holding SOC to shrink tomorrow's retail grid-charge.

**2026-05-22b** - Overnight-bridge reserve when multi-day reserve is deferred. User: pre-publish plan ended the night at ~11% SOC. Cause: before tomorrow's prices publish (~13:00 Berlin) the schedule horizon ends ~23:45 local and the multi-day post-schedule reserve is deferred (it would be a blind day-after bet), so _postSchedLoadKwh=0 and the end-of-schedule floor collapsed to MIN+8 (~13%). Phase 3d evening feed-in then drained to that floor, selling the marginal ~14ct late-evening tail and emptying the battery with ~5.5h of darkness still ahead -> morning rebuy risk. Fix: in the deferred branch, still reserve the OVERNIGHT BRIDGE - a known, non-blind quantity. Walk hour-by-hour from schedule-end to the next sunrise summing load (PV~=0 overnight) and hold it as the floor. Live effect: end-of-night SOC 11%->19%, the 14ct tail feed-ins (21:30/21:45) demoted to compensate, all 20-22ct peaks retained. Only the deferred (pre-publish) path is affected; the post-publish multi-day reserve walk is unchanged. No morning-charge regression (Phase 3c still skips all slots during preemptive).

**2026-05-22** - No grid-charge on glut mornings (preemptive days). User: battery grid-charged 2x this morning (03:00 + 05:15 UTC, ~26ct effective) right before a 749 sun-min day. Preemptive was active (target 30%, 11 neg slots) and correctly drained the evening, but overnight SOC dipped below MIN+5 and Phase 3c grid-charged to defend the floor. The night-charge skip gated on (!isDaylight && pvPower<200): isDaylight flips at civil sunrise (~05:00 local, hours before PV ramps), so pre-PV slots slipped through; tightening the gate to pvPower<load just moved the charge to the next eligible slot. Final fix: during preemptive, Phase 3c plans NO grid charge at all (midday PV refills for free), and the Phase 4 safety override is suppressed while pvPower<load - so SOC coasts to ~MIN and the household draws grid directly instead of round-trip-charging the battery. Verified via offline harness replaying today's real data at a mocked 01:00 clock: pre-fix 2 charges -> post-fix 0, min SOC 5.9% then PV recovers. Negative-price profit charging is unaffected (Phase 3f, not 3c).

**2026-05-21** - Overnight-survival guard on feed-in. Phase 3d on a sunny-tomorrow day (749 sun-min, 11 neg-price slots) drained the evening peak to ~MIN+5 because tomorrow free PV was the assumed round-trip replacement (pvOpportunityPrice ~5.7ct). The forward-validate only checked each feed-in slot own SOC against MIN+5, ignoring the overnight household load that keeps draining after the last feed-in - so SOC slid 10%->5% overnight and Phase 4 fired 4 emergency grid charges at ~27ct effective, buying back what was sold at 15-23ct. New guard walks the whole SOC trajectory and, while the trough is below MIN+5, demotes the lowest-mp feed-in slot at/before the trough and re-walks. Drops the marginal 15-16ct feed-ins that force the rebuy; keeps the 22-23ct peaks that PV genuinely refills. Structural (load-only) troughs leave Phase 4 to charge as needed.

**2026-05-20 (b)** — Phase 3c grid-charge now prefers slots at/after the ~13:00 Berlin price publish. Even after the reserve-defer fix above, one floor-protection charge still landed at 10:30 (cheapest slot, eff 10.70ct) because the battery dips to the 10% floor by tomorrow midnight. Charging before 13:00 is a blind bet on next-day prices that haven't published; afternoon slots are only marginally pricier (~13:45 eff 10.91ct, +0.2ct). Eligible-slot sort now orders post-13:00 first, pre-13:00 as fallback so a genuine morning deficit is still covered. Net: grid charging shifts to the afternoon once prices are known.

**2026-05-20** — Multi-day reserve guard: `pricesCoverTomorrow` threshold 25h→30h (optimizer). An evening run (~20:45 Berlin) saw prices ~27h out — past the 25h cutoff — so the post-horizon reserve fired and committed ~8 kWh of ~11ct effective grid-charge into tomorrow-morning slots (e.g. 10:30 @ 0.30ct market / 10.7ct effective) before the day-after prices had even published. Those prices only cover *tomorrow*, not the day-after the reserve bets on. 30h defers the reserve until prices extend into the day-after (true only after the next ~13:00 publish); tomorrow's post-13:00 prices are still cheap, so waiting costs nothing. Phase 3d feed-in guard (`_lastSchedTime`, 25h) left unchanged.

**2026-05-19 (later 5)** — FEEDIN_ROUNDTRIP_MARGIN_CT 10→5. User asked to capture today's 17:45 (mp 22.39 ct) feed-in. The early pass (post the position-guard fix) uses strict `minReplaceEff` = 16.90 ct (tomorrow's 11:15 UTC slot, mp 6.50 + 10.4 reduced fee). With margin 10 the check is 22.39 > 26.90 → blocked. Lowering to 5 makes the check 22.39 > 21.90 → passes by 0.49 ct. Real round-trip: revenue 22.39 ct minus 18.59 ct refill cost (1.10 kWh at 16.90 to put 1 kWh in battery after 10% inverter loss) minus ~1.5 ct cycle wear = ~2.3 ct/kWh net profit. Margin 10 was set on 2026-05-19 (later 2) to compensate for pvOpportunityPrice ~11 ct being applied globally — now that the early pass uses strict minReplaceEff (2026-05-19 later 4), margin 5 is the right calibration. Also pulls in 4-5 additional tomorrow-evening slots (late pass uses replacementPriceLate = pvOpp 11.23; threshold 16.23 → picks 16.95-18.75 ct range).

Follow-up same run: merged the two-pass loop into a single-pass with `continue` on round-trip failure. The two-pass order (late first) was exhausting feedinBudgetSoc on lower-mp tomorrow-evening slots before today's higher-mp 22.39 ct could be picked. Single-pass with mp DESC + per-slot replacement (early uses strict minReplaceEff, late uses replacementPriceLate) gives globally optimal ordering: today's 22.39 wins first, then tomorrow-evening fills the rest of the budget. `continue` (not `break`) is correct because per-slot check differs by idx — a high-mp early slot failing its strict check shouldn't block a lower-mp late slot from passing its looser check.

**2026-05-19 (later 4)** — Reserve walk nets PV surplus (option 2 of post-sched-PV question). Continuing the prior fix: after enabling tomorrow-evening feed-in via post-schedule pvWillCurtail (2026-05-19 later 3), Phase 3c bumped from 17→18 noon grid charges and SOC peaked at 100% before Phase 3d drained it back to 58% — a partial round-trip that lost ~€0.05/day to inverter+cycle overhead. Root cause: the reserve walk's `Math.max(0, load-pv)` accumulated deficit-only and never discounted the 22nd's 694 sun-min PV surplus against the 21st's deficit. `_postSchedLoadKwh` pinned near the 21 kWh cap, forcing endFloorSoc=79% and triggering excessive Phase 3c picks. Replaced with a running net walk (peak cumulative `(load-pv)` floored at 0) — net surplus on the 22nd subtracts from prior accumulated deficit, so _postSchedLoadKwh reflects actual reserve needed rather than worst-case deficit-only accounting. Floor at 0 preserves the safety invariant: we can't pre-charge from future PV.

Additionally: tightened Phase 3d's early-pass replacement to strict `minReplacementEffPrice` (no pvOpportunityPrice bypass). The global pvWillCurtail flag was true during this same run because today afternoon's PV-only walk overflowed to 100% — that made the early pass pick today's 17:45 (mp 22.39) since replacement dropped to pvOpp ~10 ct. But today's evening drain is replaced by tomorrow's planned grid charges, not by past PV curtailment. pvOpp stays available for the late pass (post-grid-charge slots), where it's economically correct (post-schedule PV is the actual replacement). See [[feedback_roundtrip_feedin]].

**2026-05-19 (later 3)** — Phase 3d: post-schedule PV overflow walk + position-guarded two-pass selection. User flagged: schedule ends tomorrow night at SOC 85-86% on a bad-PV day (74 sun-min), but day-after-tomorrow is 694 sun-min — feeding in at tomorrow's evening peak (mp 16-18.75 ct) makes sense because the 22nd's PV will refill what we drain. The in-horizon `pvOnlyOverflow` walk ends at schedule end (~23:45 tomorrow), so it never sees the 22nd refill — `pvWillCurtail=false` and the round-trip check uses `minReplacementEffPrice=18.69 ct` (margin 10 → need mp > 28.7), blocking every tomorrow-evening slot. Added a 48h post-schedule PV-only SOC walk that accumulates `postSchedPvOverflow` when projected SOC exceeds 99% on the 21st/22nd. To avoid re-enabling today's feed-in (which would be replaced by tomorrow's planned grid-charges, not by post-sched PV), gated the extension behind a position guard: slots after `lastPlannedChargeIdx` use `replacementPriceLate` (=pvOpportunityPrice when postSchedPvWillCurtail) and `eligibleOverflowLate` (pvOnlyOverflow + postSchedPvOverflow); earlier slots keep the strict in-horizon `replacementPrice` and `pvOnlyOverflow`. Implemented as a two-pass loop to preserve the existing 'break on first round-trip fail' semantics within each pass. See [[feedback_roundtrip_feedin]] and [[feedback_multiday_reserve]].

**2026-05-19 (later 2)** — FEEDIN_ROUNDTRIP_MARGIN_CT 5→10. After restoring the multi-day reserve walk (f37e741) the schedule still planned 2 evening feed-in slots today (17:45/18:00 UTC, mp 22.39/20.90 ct) while Phase 3c separately planned 4 grid-charge slots tomorrow noon (mp 3.05–3.89 ct ≈ 14–16 ct effective). The cheapest unplanned PV-deficit slot was ~14.9 ct effective, so the round-trip check `22.39 > 14.9 + 5 = 19.9` passed by ~2.5 ct — thinner than the real ~4 ct/kWh round-trip overhead (10% inverter loss + cycle wear). User: "discharging around 17:00 makes no sense — you have to charge tomorrow too much." Raising the margin to 10 ct demands a clearly positive trade: today's 17:45 (22.39 vs 14.9 + 10 = 24.9) and 18:00 (20.90 vs 24.9) both should break — but they didn't. Debug trace revealed `pvOnlyOverflow=0.6%` was enough to flip `pvWillCurtail` to true, dropping replacement to `pvOpportunityPrice=11.23ct` so `22.39 > 11.23 + 10 = 21.23` still passed. Fix: tightened `pvWillCurtail` to require ≥ 5 % SOC (~1.5 kWh) of PV-only overflow before the PV-opportunity replacement applies. On the bad-PV day both feed-in slots now break against the real grid-refill cost (~18.69 ct effective). See [[feedback_roundtrip_feedin]].

**2026-05-19 (later)** — Restored optimizer from silent live-flow regression. Live flow had reverted to the pre-f852733 reserve walk: `RESERVE_HOURS=14`, `MAX_RESERVE_KWH=6`, no `pricesCoverTomorrow` deferral. Effect: today's predicted schedule planned 8 feed-in slots (mp 9.71–22.39 ct) draining SOC 96→63% by midnight, then a state-1 grid charge at 21:45 UTC tomorrow (mp 13.29 ct → ~26ct effective) once SOC slid to 6%. Tomorrow's 7-day sunshine forecast is 74 sun-min (vs. 700+ on good days), so the calibrated 48h/21kWh walk would have surfaced the multi-day hole and blocked these feed-in picks. PUT live optimizer.func back to the committed f852733 body (no code-level change to battery_controller_v2.json beyond this changelog entry). Same regression class as the 53d3807 → dca6560 cycle on 2026-05-18 (see entry above) and the [[feedback_multiday_reserve]] memory.

**2026-05-19** — Phase 3c: defer multi-day reserve until tomorrow's prices publish. Added a `pricesCoverTomorrow` check (lastSchedTime − now > 25h) around the existing post-horizon reserve walk; when prices don't yet cover tomorrow, `_postSchedLoadKwh` stays 0 and Phase 3c's endTarget drops to MIN_SOC+5 (10%). In-horizon deficit picks still fire normally. User report tonight (01:00 Berlin): with reserve active, planner picked 25 mid-day grid-charge slots at ~18ct effective to hit endTarget 80% — a blind bet on tomorrow's prices that weren't loaded yet. After fix on the same run: `Phase 3c: picks=0 endTarget=10.0% endTraj=25.8% peakTraj=69.4% [reserve deferred: pre-publish]`. Once EPEX publishes (~13:00 Berlin) and the horizon extends past 25h, the reserve re-engages and the planner picks cheapest across the full 36h — naturally choosing today's vs. tomorrow's slots without a separate post-horizon walk needed. Reverts dca6560's aggressive overnight grid-charging while preserving the multi-day calibration for runs that do see tomorrow's prices.

**2026-05-18** — Restored multi-day reserve calibration. RESERVE_HOURS 14→48, MAX_RESERVE_KWH 6→21 (~70% SOC cap on 30 kWh). Accidentally reverted in 53d3807 (2026-05-10 Phase 3d pvOnlyOverflow gating) — the commit message did not mention this change and the regressed values were the original initial-commit defaults. User flagged: tonight at SOC 82% the optimizer planned a 16ct feed-in tomorrow morning + drain to 14% by end of day, while 7d forecast shows weak PV both tomorrow (177 min sunshine total) and day-after (59 min) — a guaranteed sell-low/buy-high. The 48h/21kWh calibration was originally chosen exactly to surface this multi-day PV-desert pattern (see 2026-05-09 entry).

**2026-05-14** — Phase 3d round-trip margin. Added FEEDIN_ROUNDTRIP_MARGIN_CT = 5: non-overflow feed-in candidates now require `s.marketPrice > replacementPrice + 5` instead of just `> replacementPrice`. The bare mp-vs-effectivePrice comparison ignored inverter round-trip losses (~10%) and battery cycle wear, so a sub-5ct spread booked as profit was actually a net loss — live example: planned feed-in tonight 20:30/21:00 at mp 17.9ct against tomorrow's grid-recharge effective ~14.7ct on a zero-PV day. With the 5ct margin the threshold becomes ~19.7ct and both slots are correctly demoted to compensate.

**2026-05-13** — Restored Phase 3d blind-window guard (regression from 2c34724 "Per-day PV baseline + closest-K historical matching" on 2026-05-12, which silently dropped the `blindToTomorrow` term added in c63103a one day earlier). Re-adds `pricesCoverTomorrow` (=`lastSchedTime - now > 25h`), tail-sampled `_tomorrowPvRatioP3d`, and `blindToTomorrow = !pricesCoverTomorrow && ratio < 0.60`; restores `(blindToTomorrow || s.marketPrice <= replacementPrice) → break` in the candidate loop. Without the guard, Phase 3d uses today's cheap PV-opportunity replacement price even when tomorrow's prices haven't published and tomorrow's PV forecast is 0 — exactly the scenario user flagged: morning feed-in at 14–15 ct mp followed by next-day grid charge at retail with no PV refill. Test 8/9 (same scenario 3 contrived 8h-horizon failure as c63103a; production runs are unaffected).

**2026-05-12** — Per-day PV baseline + closest-K historical matching. Old code computed a single `midDaySunRatio` from the 7-day MAX of `sunshineDurationInMinutes`, so every day in the horizon shared one baseline calibrated to the brightest hour anywhere in the window — sunny days underestimated, cloudy days got noise. Replaced with `getDayBaseline(t)`: per-day sunRatio (from forecast), per-day historical matching via top-K (K=5) closest-peak days. `refRatio` now reflects the matched set's actual peak rather than the slot's target ratio, so per-slot scaling anchors correctly. Live: tomorrow's forecast (40 min/h peak, 434 min total) was 14.4 kWh in the schedule vs. 20.8 kWh weather7days; today's overcast day was sharing the same baseline as tomorrow. Regression 9/9 pass.

**2026-05-11** — Phase 3d blind-window guard: block non-overflow feed-in when tomorrow's prices aren't loaded yet (lastSchedTime - now ≤ 25h) AND tomorrow's PV forecast ratio < 0.60. Closes recurring sell-low/buy-high loss (morning sell @ 13–15ct → next-day grid-charge @ ~15.5ct effective). Overflow exemption preserved.

### 2026-05-10 (Phase 3c: cap grid-charge SOC at 90%)
- User: midday charge cluster fills battery to 99.5% from grid. Two
  problems: (a) the 3 most-expensive picks land at 09:45/10:15/10:45
  UTC (11:45/12:15/12:45 Berlin) — BEFORE 13:00 Berlin when the next
  day's prices publish, so we commit to charging without knowing if
  tomorrow's slots will be cheaper; (b) routinely cycling to 100%
  via grid increases wear.
- Fix: introduced `MAX_GRID_CHARGE_SOC_PCT = 90`. Phase 3c's
  `simulateSocTrajectory` clamps at 90 instead of 100. Once the
  projection hits the cap, additional charge picks raise traj[d] by
  0 → the "pick raised SOC?" gate fails → Phase 3c stops adding
  slots. Cheapest-first ordering means the dropped slots are the
  most expensive (the pre-13:00 morning ones here). PV-driven fill
  above 90 is still allowed at runtime — only the PLANNER refuses
  to schedule grid slots past the cap, so PV-glut days aren't
  curtailed.
- End-of-horizon SOC: ~70-75% (down from ~82%) — closer to the
  raw reserve target (~80%) but below it; trade-off accepted for
  reduced wear and the pre-13:00 avoidance.
- Regression 9/9 pass.

### 2026-05-10 (Phase 3d: pvOnlyOverflow gates pvWillCurtail + isOverflowOnly)
- User report: tomorrow's plan feeds in at 15.75/16ct in the morning
  (05:30 & 06:00 UTC) then grid-charges 16 slots midday at mp 10–12ct.
  Round-trip = sell 15.75ct, refill at 22ct effective (mp + 10.4ct
  discount fee) → ~6ct/kWh loss. Weak-PV day (peak 657W); no actual
  PV curtailment possible.
- Root cause: Phase 3d's `pvWillCurtail` flag AND its `isOverflowOnly`
  shortcut both read from `overflowSoc`/`postHorizonOverflow`, which
  are computed by a projection that adds `_plan='charge'` slots. On
  weak-PV days Phase 3c plans aggressive midday grid charging that
  pushes projSoc past 99, so the optimizer thinks PV will curtail and
  treats the first feed-in pick as "free overflow" — bypassing the
  round-trip check.
- Fix: compute a separate `pvOnlyOverflow` from a PV–load-only walk
  (no charge/feed-in plans). Use it for BOTH `pvWillCurtail` and the
  `isOverflowOnly` shortcut in the candidate loop. The first pick now
  has to satisfy `mp > replacementPrice` like every other pick.

### 2026-05-09 (Reserve cap raised 12→21 kWh)
- User: "wenn man den strom vom grid kaufen muss ist es ganz schlecht"
  — feed-in tonight at 16ct losing money if forced to grid-charge Mon
  morning at retail (~30ct). The 12 kWh cap (40% SOC) was the binding
  constraint: walk computed ~34 kWh deficit for the dead-PV streak Mon-Tue
  but got clipped at 12. End-SOC tomorrow only rose to 50% — still below
  the daily-load envelope (~17 kWh ≈ 57% SOC).
- Fix: MAX_RESERVE_KWH 12 → 21 kWh (~70% SOC reserve). End-of-horizon
  target now climbs to ~80% on dead-PV forecasts, which blocks almost all
  feed-in tonight (SOC 86% → ~80% = ~2 kWh budget). Cap is non-binding
  on good-weather forecasts because the walk already weighs PV via
  getSunshineForecast — sunshine eats most of the deficit on sunny days.

### 2026-05-09 (Multi-day post-horizon reserve)
- User report: optimizer planned 13 kWh feed-in tonight (mp 14-17ct)
  draining SOC 86% → 31%, with end-of-tomorrow target 30%. But weather
  forecast shows Mon-Sat all <2h sunshine each (heavy rain). Tomorrow's
  modest PV refill (~20 kWh) won't last the streak; would force grid-
  charge at peak Mon morning. Root cause: post-schedule reserve walk only
  covered 14h and capped at 6 kWh (~20% SOC). The slightly-negative
  tomorrow prices (-0.24ct min) tripped the "strong solar" rule and set
  end-of-horizon target to 30%, with no signal that the day after was a
  PV desert.
- Fix: extended reserve walk RESERVE_HOURS 14 → 48 and raised cap
  MAX_RESERVE_KWH 6 → 12 (40% SOC on a 30 kWh battery). The walk already
  weighs PV via getSunshineForecast (forecastnew has 7d hourly data), so
  good-weather days don't pad the reserve — only sustained PV holes do.
  When the next 2 days look bad, end-slot target rises (minTargetSoc +
  reserve%), which feeds back to feed-in budget (line 936) and runtime
  socNeeded (line 1330), blocking aggressive overnight feed-in.

### 2026-05-09 (Phase 3a: DP-based preemptive pick)
- User report: 06:30/06:45 fed in at mp 13.51/12.85 ct while 08:00
  (mp 13.98 ct, the day's HIGHEST) only held (state=3). Root cause:
  preemptive sorted candidates by mp DESC and committed top-N without
  modeling chronological SOC depletion. Phase 4's runtime gate
  `soc > target+3` then demoted the high-mp later slot because earlier
  picks had drained SOC below the gate at its time. Net: the best slot
  of the day silently lost to a lower-mp earlier sibling.
- Fix: replaced sort-by-mp DESC pick with a 2D DP over (slot_idx,
  soc_bucket). Maximizes Σ(mp × reliefSoc) subject to (a) round-trip
  filter mp > minReplacementEffPrice and (b) chronological gate
  pre-soc > target+3. SOC quantized to 0.5%; ~50 slots × 200 buckets
  trivial. Removed extraDrainNeeded cap: every DP pick is round-trip
  profitable, so over-drain is net-revenue-positive (and the gate
  bounds total drain anyway). reliefSoc uses the same physical metric
  as Phase 3d (state=3 vs state=4 SOC delta), so high-PV slots with
  drainW=0 still earn their (pv-load) credit.
- Regression 9/9 pass.

### 2026-05-07b (Phase 3d: budget uses net relief, cap at 99, round-up)
- After 5/7a fix, evening peaks were picked but tomorrow midday still
  forced 1-2 curtailment slots at 6-8 ct. Three compounding errors:
  (1) Budget loop compared full feedinDrainSoc (3.5%/slot) vs projected
      overflow, but state=3 would have drained ~0.58%/slot anyway —
      effective relief per feed-in slot is only ~2.92%. Picked too few.
  (2) Projection capped overflow at 100% but the runtime curtailment
      branch fires at soc≥99 — 1% mismatch left a stray curtail slot.
  (3) Discretization: budget 19.3% / 2.92% per slot rounds DOWN to 6
      slots (17.52% relief), leaving 1.78% residual to curtail.
- Fix: (1) new feedinReliefSoc(slot) = feedinDrainSoc + (pv-load)*soc;
       (2) projection caps at 99 to align with runtime trigger;
       (3) loop break flips from "would-overshoot" to "already-met" —
           pick the slot that crosses budget if mp still beats
           replacementPrice. The overshoot is bounded by 1 slot.
- Verified live: 7 evening slots picked (mp 18.89-21.88 ct);
  tomorrow midday peaks at 97.7% SOC, ZERO curtailment slots.
  Regression 9/9 pass.

### 2026-05-07 (Phase 3d: pvWillCurtail relax PV-opportunity gate)
- Round-trip guard still blocked evening feed-in when tomorrow's
  curtailment mp was positive-but-low. Today (5/7): 17-22 ct evening
  peak skipped because cheapPvRefillAhead (mp<3) was false — tomorrow's
  midday lowest mp is 5.45 ct. replacementPrice fell back to
  minReplacementEffPrice (~24.75 ct from pre-sunrise grid recharge)
  and 21.88ct evening peak failed 21.88 > 24.75. Same SOC then dumped
  via forced curtailment at 6-7 ct.
- Fix: gate pvOpportunityPrice on (cheapPvRefillAhead || pvWillCurtail)
  where pvWillCurtail = (overflowSoc + postHorizonOverflow) > 0. When
  PV will saturate the battery anyway, the SOC drained tonight is
  displaced from a forced curtailment slot, not a hypothetical grid
  recharge — correct replacement cost is the forgone curtailment
  revenue (~7-9 ct), not pre-sunrise grid (~24 ct).
- Verified live: 4 evening slots picked at 21.88/20.32/20.33/20.13 ct;
  tomorrow's curtailment shrunk from 5 slots to 2. Regression 9/9 pass.

### 2026-05-06 (Saturation cluster + round-trip-aware runtime)
- Phase 4 runtime "battery full + PV surplus" branch was firing
  opportunistically at the FIRST slot crossing soc≥95 with mp>floor,
  ignoring round-trip economics. Today (5/6) at 16:00 it fed-in at
  mp=9.06ct (local minimum of the day) while replacement cost was
  ~22ct/kWh — a -13ct/kWh loss. The same saturation cluster contained
  17:30-17:45 at mp=13-14ct, also a loser vs replacement (weak PV
  forecast tomorrow), so the right answer was: don't feed in at all.
- Fix: hoisted `replacementPrice` out of the Phase 3d block so the
  runtime branch can use the same round-trip threshold.
- Phase 4 "battery full + PV surplus" branch now fires only when
  (a) soc≥99 (imminent curtailment, sell at any mp>FEEDIN_MIN_MP_CT)
  or (b) mp>replacementPrice (round-trip profitable). Otherwise
  state=3 (hold), preserving SOC for tomorrow's load.
- New Phase 3d.2 "Saturation cluster" pass: walks the passive
  projection (`_plannedSoc`), finds consecutive (soc≥95 + PV>load+500)
  runs, and marks the highest-mp slot per cluster as
  `feedin_saturation` IFF mp>replacementPrice. Without this, on
  profitable cluster days the runtime would still fire at the first
  cluster slot rather than the best-mp one.
- Planned-feedin branch (Phase 4) now also handles `feedin_saturation`.

### 2026-05-05 (FEEDIN_MIN_MP_CT floor)
- New constant FEEDIN_MIN_MP_CT=1 prevents feed-in (planned or runtime) at
  sub-1ct marketPrice. Today produced state=4 at mp=0.14ct (12:30 UTC,
  soc=96%, PV=4640W) via the Phase 4 "Battery full + PV surplus, mp > 0"
  branch — sub-cent revenue does not cover cycle wear / inverter losses.
  Floor applied at Phase 3d candidate filter (was mp > 0), Phase 3e
  minThreshold (was Math.max(0.01, ...)), and Phase 4 runtime branch
  (was mp > 0). Phase 3d overflow-exempt slots also affected — even
  truly curtailed PV is not worth cycling the battery for sub-cent prices
  when actual PV may underperform forecast.

### 2026-04-28 (Phase 3d: PV-refill opportunity cost)
- When schedule has cheap (mp<3) PV-surplus daylight ahead, replacement
  cost = avg PV-time market price (forgone feed-in revenue), not the
  pre-sunrise grid-charge effective price. Was blocking evening feed-in
  at 13-16c because minReplacementEffPrice computed against a hypothetical
  24c grid recharge that never happens — tomorrow's PV does the recharge
  for free. New `cheapPvRefillAhead` scans the WHOLE schedule (not just
  [0, horizonIdx)) so post-horizon PV is visible. Falls back to original
  rule on cloudy days. Min ensures we never raise the threshold.

### 2026-04-25 (Retire infra.plan)
- Removed `write_plan`, `query_prev_plan`, `set_topic_prev_plan`.
- Removed prev_plan refs from cronplus + `inject_manual`.
- `join_results.count` 9 -> 8.
- Optimizer: dropped msg2 build; output[1] now unwired
  (`return [msg, null, msg3]`). Forward-looking schedule lives in
  `forecast.batteryplan` only.

### 2026-04-25 (Forecast write: batteryplan)
- Added `to_batteryplan_points` (function) and `write_batteryplan`
  (influxdb batch -> forecast db, measurement `batteryplan`).
- Wired optimizer output[0] to the new chain alongside `debug_schedule`.
- Optimizer output gains numeric `t` (epoch ms) alongside the existing
  human-readable `time` string; new function uses `t` for the influx
  point timestamp (the localized `time` is not Date-parseable).
- Fields per slot: state, predictedSoc, marketPrice, PvPower.
- Same slot timestamp overwrites prior forecasts (forward-looking view).

### 2026-04-25 (Phase 3f: cheapest-ep selection within profit-charge stretch)
- Replaced the time-greedy runtime profit-charge branch (Phase 4) with
  Phase 3f planning. Within each ep<0 stretch, Phase 3f computes K =
  floor((100 - entrySoc) / soc_per_charge), sorts stretch slots by ep ASC,
  and marks the K cheapest-ep slots as _plan='charge'. The existing
  Phase 4 plan==='charge' branch executes them. Higher-ep slots in the
  stretch are left unplanned and fall through to the PV-surplus / curtail
  branches. Without this, the time-first runtime branch would saturate
  the battery on the early (less-negative) slots and curtail the
  later, deeper-negative slots — leaving money on the table. Forward-
  validate no longer relies on isProfitChargeSlot since _plan is now
  authoritative for charging.

### 2026-04-25 (Phase 3e: pre-discharge for profit-charge windows)
- Phase 3e plans pre-discharge feed-in BEFORE consecutive ep<0 stretches
  that would saturate the battery. Without it, paid-to-import slots after
  the SOC cap are curtailed (e.g. mid-day -19ct slots when battery filled
  to 100% by earlier neg-eff slots). Phase 3e walks the schedule, simulates
  SOC including runtime profit-charge, identifies overflow per stretch,
  and assigns 'feedin_capacity' to the highest-mp earlier unplanned slots
  until the overflow is consumed. Round-trip threshold is `mp > minEp +
  cycle_wear` (cycle_wear=3ct), which any positive mp clears for deep
  neg-eff windows. The forward-validate now also handles 'feedin_capacity'
  and models profit-charge so evening feed-ins aren't incorrectly demoted.
  isProfitChargeSlot() helper centralises the runtime-charge predicate
  (used by Phase 3c trajectory, Phase 3d projections, Phase 3e detection,
  forward-validate).

### 2026-04-25 (profit-charge at neg effective price)
- Added profit-charge branch: whenever effectivePrice < 0 and SOC < 95%
  the optimizer now goes state=1 / MAX_CHARGE_W, capturing the paid-to-
  import value regardless of future load needs. Previously grid charging
  required SOC to drop below MIN_SOC+5 future-need threshold, so deeply
  negative-price slots on sunny days produced PV-only charging while
  free-money grid intake was left on the table. Inserted ahead of the
  "Strong PV surplus" branch so PV+grid combine up to the charger limit;
  preemptive-night exclusion preserved (no grid charging at night before
  next-day solar). Feed-in-at-neg-price hard guard untouched — import
  and export sides are independent.

### 2026-04-24 (round-trip guard fix)
- Round-trip feed-in guard now excludes PV-surplus slots from the
  replacement-cost calculation in BOTH Phase 3d and preemptive.
  Previously the cheapest future effectivePrice was dragged down by
  mid-day glut slots (e.g. -9ct), but we can't actually grid-charge
  during glut — PV refills the battery for free. The true replacement
  cost is paid when PV < load (morning dead zone, late night), where
  grid serves load or recharges the battery at effective ~22ct.
  Preemptive filter now also restricts to pre-sunrise + future-only
  (was including past slots because the past-slot trim happens after
  the preemptive block).

### 2026-04-24 (preemptive round-trip guard)
- Preemptive discharge now applies the same round-trip economic check
  that Phase 3d feed-in uses. Each candidate evening slot's marketPrice
  must exceed the cheapest future effectivePrice in slots where grid
  charging is plausibly needed (pvPower < loadEst + 500W). Without the
  check, the optimizer was feeding to grid at ~11ct evening and then
  planning morning grid charges at ~22ct effective — a ~11ct/kWh loss
  per cycle. PV-glut slots are excluded from the replacement set because
  the battery can't actually be charged from them (it's filled by PV
  surplus directly).

### 2026-04-24 (target SOC raised)
- Preemptive sunrise target SOC bumped. Floor is now 30% when
  neg-prices confirm the glut (was 20%), else 40% (was 35%). The
  hard cap in the neg-price branch is now 30% (was 20%). Experience
  showed the earlier 20% target left too little morning reserve on
  mixed-forecast days; the glut day can still absorb plenty of PV
  starting from 30%.

## Changelog

### 2026-04-24 (schedule → global.pp.prediction)
- Battery Optimizer now writes its first-output schedule array
  (`output`) into `global.pp.prediction` (file store) just before
  returning, so downstream flows can consume the planned schedule
  without subscribing to the optimizer's output messages. Uses the
  `file` store to match the existing `pp` object. Reverted the
  earlier attempt that stored the raw pv_history array there.

### 2026-04-24 (later)
- Preemptive sunrise target SOC: floor raised and made conditional on
  neg-price confirmation. Was `max(15, 100-capacityNeededPct)`; now
  `max(socFloor, 100-capacityNeededPct)` where socFloor = 20 when
  negPriceSlots >= 4 (external confirmation of glut) else 35. The old
  15% floor trusted the PV forecast alone and left no buffer for the
  morning peak if PV underperformed.

### 2026-04-24
- Rolling-horizon refill detection now uses relative thresholds: flag
  `dipped` when passive SOC falls ≥5 points below its running peak, set
  horizon at first recovery within 3 points of that peak. Absolute
  70/85 thresholds missed high-starting-SOC runs where the overnight
  trough never broke 70, collapsing the feed-in budget to 0 and
  skipping legitimate evening peaks.
- Replace bare `new Date()` with `new Date(Date.now())` in the pv_history
  day-of-year filter so regression tests' Date.now mock is respected
  (V8's no-arg `new Date()` bypasses mocked Date.now).
- Scenario 1 now passes (8/8).
- Load estimator now uses 7-day history with per-hour MEDIAN across
  days (was: last 24h, single value per hour overwritten by final
  row — a single-day spike like EV charging at 07:00 poisoned every
  future 07:00 forecast). Hours with <3 samples fall back to
  AVG_LOAD_W. Also widened InfluxDB query from 24h to 7d.
- Preemptive glut day is now the current-or-next daylight period,
  so preemptive still runs when the cron fires after sunrise on
  the glut day itself (old code required a night→day transition
  and silently disabled preemptive in that case).
- Preemptive eligibility window extended from "night + pre-PV morning"
  to "now → post-glut sunrise", sorted by price DESC, so the morning
  peak wins over sub-1ct pre-dawn slots.
- Preemptive drain sizing projects SOC with PV+load (+ overflow above
  100%) across the window instead of assuming load-only.
- Feed-in budget projection continues past horizonIdx through the next
  daylight period to catch PV curtailment after the refill slot.
- Added regression scenarios 7 and 8.

### 2026-04-17
- Phase 3d: add round-trip economic check on planned feed-in.
  Non-overflow feed-in now requires marketPrice > cheapest future
  effectivePrice (marketPrice + gridFee) among unplanned slots.
  Overflow (curtailment) SOC remains exempt — pure revenue.
  Fixes: evening feed-in followed by morning grid-charge at a net loss.

name: to batteryplan points
